definition: Current aggregate hyperscaler capex/OCF at 50-65%, approaching generalist_02's kill-zone dual threshold: ROIC below WACC for 4+ quarters AND capex/OCF >55-65%. At 30-50% capex growth rates, 2-3 hyperscalers could cross by late 2027. Meta and Amazon already at or near 70% capex/OCF. Thresholds derived from 5 historical analogues, though 4 of 5 were debt-funded (structurally different), suggesting self-funded entities may tolerate higher ratios.
evidence: Dual threshold derived from 5 historical analogues
At 30-50% capex growth rates, 2-3 hyperscalers could cross by late 2027
Meta and Amazon already at or near 70% capex/OCF
4 of 5 analogues were debt-funded — structural difference challenged in pair_0
open_questions: Is the kill-zone threshold higher for self-funded entities?
Does the prisoner's dilemma in capex rationalization prevent individual firms from pulling back first?
What signals management language shifts on capex discipline?
